channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: College football coach fired for refusing COVID vaccine loses lawsuit after Obama-appointed judge's ruling
item.description: Former Washington State head football coach Nick Rolovich has lost his lawsuit against the university over his firing for refusing the COVID-19 vaccine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Raiders fire coach Antonio Pierce after he goes 4-13 in lone full season
item.description: HENDERSON, Nev. (AP) — The Las Vegas Raiders fired Antonio Pierce on Tuesday after just one season as their full-time coach, the latest in a line of coaching changes over the past several years.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Elon Musk has expressed interest in buying English soccer club Liverpool, his father says
item.description: Elon Musk's father says his billionaire son has expressed interest in buying English soccer club Liverpool. The Premier League club is privately owned by Fenway Sports Group, which has not indicated it wants to sell but has accepted external investment in the past.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UConn guard Paige Bueckers has a left knee sprain, could return in a week
item.description: STORRS, Conn. (AP) — UConn guard Paige Bueckers has a left knee sprain, avoiding a serious injury. She will miss Wednesday's game against Xavier. Bueckers injured the knee in the third quarter of UConn's win over Villanova on Sunday. Coach Geno Auriemma said she may miss a few games.
item.dc:creator: JIM FULLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Black coaches have shot at college football title a generation after that milestone was hit in hoops
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — In college basketball, the scene of an African American coach cutting down the nets on their way to a national title is not new, even if it is still a relative rarity.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Gaudreau suspect pleads not guilty to homicide charges after turning down 35-year prison sentence
item.description: The driver charged with killing NHL hockey player Johnny Gaudreau and his brother, Matthew, as they cycled on a rural New Jersey road has pleaded not guilty after turning down a proposed plea agreement.
item.dc:creator: Maryclaire Dale, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Chargers to sign Ezekiel Elliott ahead of playoff game: reports
item.description: The Los Angeles Chargers are set to sign veteran running back Ezekiel Elliott to their practice squad ahead of their playoff game against the Houston Texans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The 49ers fire special teams coordinator Brian Schneider, AP source says
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — The San Francisco 49ers fired special teams coach Brian Schneider following a season when repeated blunders in the kicking contributed to a losing season, a person familiar with the decision told The Associated Press on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sheel Seidler, wife of late Padres owner, sues in-laws for control of the team
item.description: The wife of late San Diego Padres owner Peter Seidler, Sheel Seidler, sued brothers-in-law Matthew and Robert on Monday, attempting to prevent another brother, John, from taking control of the team rather than her.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Report: Bears seek permission to interview Cowboys' Mike McCarthy for head coaching vacancy
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago Bears have sought permission to interview Dallas Cowboys head coach Mike McCarthy and are awaiting a response from the Cowboys, according to ESPN's Adam Schefter.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Biden signs RFK Stadium land bill into law, a step toward potential Commanders stadium in Washington
item.description: President Joe Biden signed into law a bill that transfers the land that includes the old RFK Stadium from the federal government to city control, the next step to potentially pave the way for the NFL's Washington Commanders to return to the nation's capital.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Patriots owner Robert Kraft says he put Jerod Mayo in 'untenable situation,' vows to correct course
item.description: FOXBOROUGH, Mass. (AP) — When Robert Kraft hired Jerod Mayo a year ago, he felt he’d identified the right person to follow the championship legacy left by Bill Belichick. In hindsight, Kraft now believes Mayo wasn’t quite ready to be an NFL head coach.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jaguars fire coach Doug Pederson, keep GM Trent Baalke after 'best team assembled' wins just 4 games
item.description: JACKSONVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Jacksonville Jaguars coach Doug Pederson and general manager Trent Baalke never developed enough synergy, if any at all. Pederson thought talent was Jacksonville's bigger issue as losses mounted; Baalke believed coaching was the problem.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WWE announces location of 2026 Royal Rumble
item.description: WWE announced on Monday the Royal Rumble will take place outside of North America for the first time when Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, plays host in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tyreek Hill channels Antonio Brown after remarks about future with Dolphins
item.description: Miami Dolphins wide receiver Tyreek Hill channeled his inner Antonio Brown with a profile picture change a day after he suggested he wanted to leave the team.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Central football team wins third Division III national title, finishes season a perfect 15-0
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — The North Central football dynasty has reached rarified air. So has quarterback Luke Lehnen.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Lions secure NFC's No. 1 seed, Broncos and Buccaneers clinch the NFL's final two playoff spots
item.description: The road to the Super Bowl in the NFC will go through Motown. The Detroit Lions secured a No. 1 seed with a 31-9 victory over the Minnesota Vikings on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sensabaugh stays hot as Jazz beat Orlando, 105-92
item.description: ORLANDO (ABC4 Sports) - So far, 2025 has treated the Utah Jazz pretty well, especially Brice Sensabaugh.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Colts owner Jim Irsay says he's retaining GM Chris Ballard and coach Shane Steichen
item.description: About four hours after the Colts completed their season with a 26-23 overtime victory over the Jacksonville Jaguars, Irsay ended any speculation about possible offseason changes by announcing in a letter to fans that longtime general manager Chris Ballard and second-year coach Shane Steichen would r
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: San Diego Wave FC announces termination of contract for midfielder Emily van Egmond
item.description: San Diego Wave FC announced Friday that midfielder Emily van Egmond and the club have mutually agreed to terminate her contract.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, Fox 5

item.title: Browns fire offensive coordinator Ken Dorsey and O-line coach Andy Dickerson after 3-14 season
item.description: Hours after finishing 3-14 with a loss in the finale of a season that was expected to carry into the playoffs, Cleveland coach Kevin Stefanski fired offensive coordinator Ken Dorsey and offensive line coach Andy Dickerson.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bulls to retire Derrick Rose's No. 1 jersey
item.description: CHICAGO — The Chicago Bulls announced Saturday that the team would be retiring Derrick Rose’s No. 1 jersey next season. Bulls President & CEO Michael Reinsdorf made the announcement on Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, WGN 9

item.title: Utah's road winning streak ends in Seattle
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) - Jaden Schwartz had a goal and two assists for the Seattle Kraken, who pulled away in the third period for a 5-2 win against the Utah Hockey Club at Climate Pledge Arena on Monday. The loss ends Utah's seven-game road winning streak. “[Points] never come easy,” Schwartz said.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Holiday Classic: Father Judge 80, Carlsbad 70
item.description: In the Third-Place game for the National Bracket of the 2024 Holiday Classic, Father Judge High School from Philadelphia, PA defeats Carlsbad 80-70.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5
